_comment: 2D sweep: rocking frequency (omega_b) × maximum rocking angle (theta_max).
Scientific basis
Kim et al. (2025) swept f_b and theta_max independently (OPAT): 10 frequency
values at theta_max=7° and 6 angle values at f_b=32.5 rpm.  This sweep fills
in the full 10×6 factorial, revealing:
- Interaction effects: does the resonance at f_b≈22.5 rpm persist at small
angles (theta_max≤3°)?  Does the turbulent transition at 37.5 rpm shift
when the forcing amplitude decreases?
- The kLa–shear Pareto frontier in (f_b, theta_max) space: both quantities
increase monotonically along each OPAT axis; the 2D map shows whether
off-diagonal combinations offer a better trade-off.
- The small-angle vortex anomaly (extra counter-rotating vortices at
theta_max≤3°, observed in the paper) across all frequencies.
Parameter limits
omega_b: same 10-point grid as sweep_fb_fill.json [15–37.5 rpm].
theta_max: paper's 6-point angle grid [2°–7°].
Lower bound 2°: paper's lower limit; below 2° the sloshing is quasi-static
and the code's ramp time dominates over the measurement window.
Upper bound 7°: paper's upper limit; above 7° wave breaking and interface–
wall contact become likely (contact-angle model not yet implemented).
fill_level: fixed at 0.5 (paper's value, enables direct comparison).
geometry: paper's 2L cellbag rectangle.
Expansion: cartesian product (10 × 6 = 60 runs).
Checkpoint groups: 6 groups × 10 segments, grouped by theta_max[0].
Within each group, segments chain from low→high f_b at fixed angle,
using smooth-step angular interpolation across the transition.
6 chains run in parallel on SLURM — total wall clock ≈ 10 × walltime.
